description: Address semantic review findings: install DashboardTracingLayer, fix TPS calculation, add empty password validation, use process RSS for memory, add WebSocket audit logging, document ban persistence gap, and add session expiry TODO.
steps: Step 1 - Install DashboardTracingLayer: Create a global CONSOLE_BROADCAST static in pumpkin/src/lib.rs, add the DashboardTracingLayer to the tracing subscriber registry in init_logger, and use the shared static when spawning the dashboard.
Step 2 - Fix TPS calculation: In pumpkin/src/net/dashboard.rs, compute actual TPS from avg_tick_ms rather than always returning the configured value.
Step 3 - Empty password validation: In PumpkinServer::new(), check that the dashboard password is non-empty before starting. Log an error and skip startup if empty.
Step 4 - Process RSS for memory: In pumpkin/src/net/dashboard.rs, use sysinfo process-specific memory instead of system-wide used_memory.
Step 5 - WebSocket audit logging: In pumpkin-dashboard/src/api/console.rs, add tracing::info when a command is received via WebSocket.
Step 6 - Ban persistence warning: In pumpkin/src/net/dashboard.rs ban_player, add a tracing::warn noting ban persistence is not yet implemented.
Step 7 - Session expiry TODO: Add a doc comment on sessions field in AppState noting that session expiry should be added in a future iteration.
acceptance_criteria: DashboardTracingLayer is registered in the tracing subscriber so WebSocket clients receive log lines
TPS in get_status reflects actual tick performance, not just the configured value
Dashboard refuses to start if password is empty, with an error log
Memory usage reports process RSS, not system-wide RAM
WebSocket command execution is audit-logged with tracing::info
Ban function logs a warning about persistence gap
Sessions field documents the need for future expiry logic
verification: Verify CONSOLE_BROADCAST static exists in pumpkin/src/lib.rs
Verify DashboardTracingLayer is added to registry in init_logger
Verify TPS is computed from avg_tick_ms in get_status
Verify password check in PumpkinServer::new dashboard startup
Verify process memory usage in get_status
Verify doc comment on sessions field
